# Wiz Integrates with Notion Custom Agents via MCP | Wiz Blog

Notion Custom Agents
are shared AI teammates that automate work across your workspace using instructions, permissions, and connections to the tools your team already relies on.
With the
Wiz MCP server
,
Custom Agents can securely access Wiz cloud security insights — helping teams answer security questions, generate reports, and investigate risks directly inside Notion. Current Wiz users can learn how to set this up in the
Wiz Docs Page
.
This means security context no longer lives only inside security tools. Instead, it can appear where teams already plan, collaborate, and make decisions. Notion Custom Agents are one example of how Wiz MCP helps customers unlock custom workflows and bring security insights into the environments where they work and build.
Bringing Wiz security insights directly into everyday workflows
Connecting Wiz to
Notion Custom Agents
changes how security information reaches teams across the organization.
Instead of switching between tools or manually compiling updates, Custom Agents can pull trusted security context from Wiz and deliver it directly inside the workflows where teams already collaborate.
With Wiz connected through
MCP
, teams can:
Answer security questions without leaving Notion
Ask about cloud resources, exposures, or risks and receive responses grounded in Wiz’s
cloud security graph
.
Automate recurring security reporting
Generate scheduled summaries of critical vulnerabilities and risks, keeping stakeholders informed without manual effort.
Share actionable context across teams
Deliver prioritized insights directly into shared workspaces, helping security, engineering, and platform teams stay aligned.
Reduce context switching during investigations
Bring relevant security findings into planning and collaboration workflows instead of moving between dashboards.
Security insights become part of how work happens — not another destination teams need to check.
What this looks like in practice
Security insights become significantly more useful when they appear directly inside the workflows teams already use. With Notion Custom Agents connected through Wiz MCP, teams can turn cloud security context into automated, collaborative workflows inside their workspace.
The following examples show how teams can use Custom Agents to bring Wiz insights into everyday work.
Wiz Cloud Questioner
The
Wiz Cloud Questioner
allows teams to ask questions about their cloud environment directly inside Notion and receive answers powered by Wiz insights.
Instead of navigating multiple security dashboards, users can ask:
Which resources are currently exposed?
What risks affect this environment?
Where should remediation be prioritized?
The Custom Agent securely queries Wiz and returns contextual answers grounded in real relationships between infrastructure, identities, and risk.
Wiz Vulnerability Summarizer
Security reporting is often repetitive and time-consuming. The
Wiz Vulnerability Summarizer
automates this process by generating recurring summaries of critical and
high-severity vulnerabilities
directly from Wiz.
These summaries can be delivered automatically into shared Notion pages, helping stakeholders stay informed and aligned without additional effort.
See it in action
Bringing security insights to where work happens
MCP brings Wiz security insights directly into where work happens — including workflows teams automate and AI experiences teams build.
Notion Custom Agents are a clear example of this in practice. With
MCP Connections built directly into Custom Agents
, Notion enables agents to securely connect to external systems like Wiz. By connecting Wiz through MCP, teams can pull real cloud security context directly into their Notion workspace, allowing reporting, investigation, and collaboration to happen alongside the work itself.
MCP enables teams to use Wiz insights in a variety of ways, including:
Automating operational workflows
Trigger reports, updates, or actions based on real-time security findings without manual coordination.
Supporting investigation and remediation workflows
Bring Wiz context directly into the tools teams use to analyze and resolve issues.
Powering custom AI assistants and internal automations
Build AI-driven experiences that can query Wiz intelligence to answer questions, summarize risk, and guide decisions using trusted cloud context.
Together, these experiences reflect a simple goal: making security insights available wherever teams already collaborate and build.
Try it out
We’re excited to be a featured launch partner for Notion Custom Agents, now available in public beta.
Get started by:
Trying
Notion Custom Agents
and the Wiz-powered templates available in the Notion Marketplace.
Learning more about
Wiz MCP
and how it helps bring security insights into the workflows and AI experiences you build.
